hooks.command:
bash -c 'FILE=$(cat | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty"); EXT="${FILE##*.}"; if [[ "$EXT" =~ ^(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$ ]] && [ -f "$FILE" ]; then ISSUES=""; if grep -qn ": any" "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; then ISSUES="${ISSUES}⚠️ TIPO ANY trovato - usa tipo esplicito
"; fi; if grep -qn "console\.log" "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; then ISSUES="${ISSUES}⚠️ console.log trovato - rimuovere per produzione
"; fi; if grep -Pn "dangerouslySetInnerHTML.*\$\{" "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; then ISSUES="${ISSUES}🔴 dangerouslySetInnerHTML con variabile utente!
"; fi; if [ -n "$ISSUES" ]; then echo -e "
🔍 QUALITY CHECK:
$ISSUES" >&2; fi; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'CMD=$(cat | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "git push.*--force|rm -rf /|git reset --hard"; then echo "🔴 COMANDO PERICOLOSO BLOCCATO: $CMD" >&2; echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"Comando distruttivo bloccato\"}" ; else echo "{\"decision\": \"allow\"}" ; fi'

hooks.prompt: Quick check (only if .ts/.tsx files were edited): 1) Was 'npm run lint' run? 2) Was 'npm run build' run if deployment requested? For config/md changes these checks are NOT required. Do NOT block — just remind if applicable.
